summary: Russian forces continue multi-front offensive operations with drone strikes across Ukraine and artillery assaults on urban centers. Ukraine faces severe energy infrastructure strain from both Russian attacks and record heatwave-driven consumption. The commander of Ukraine's 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade was found dead under investigation.
key_points: Russian forces liberated Bogodarovka in the Dnepropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in eastern Zaporozhye region, advancing along the Vovcha River bed. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31243)
A Russian KAB strike on Kharkiv killed a 23-year-old woman and wounded 10 people, damaging a tram, power network, over 15 vehicles, and nearby buildings. (Source: @UkraineNow/82267)

Ukraine will implement scheduled power outages across all regions tomorrow due to extreme heatwave-driven energy consumption spikes and equipment under repair after Russian attacks. (Source: @UkraineNow/82272)
The commander of Ukraine's 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade, Vladimir Kononnikov, was found dead; the 'South' operational command is investigating the cause. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31226)
Russian aviation struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces deployment point in Nikolayevka, DNR, with two FAB-1500 bombs according to the Russian Defense Ministry. (Source: @tass_agency/382934)
A fire broke out at an oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai from falling UAV debris, according to the regional operational headquarters. (Source: @tass_agency/382937)
Massive power outages hit occupied Donetsk, Kherson region, and Crimea after a suspected fire at the 'Kuban' energy transition point supplying power to Crimea. (Source: @UkraineNow/82248)
Russian artillery destroyed a Ukrainian military shelter in Konstantinovka, where Russian forces continue urban assault operations. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31242)

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces continue multi-front offensive operations with drone strikes across Ukraine and artillery assaults on urban centers. Ukraine faces severe energy infrastructure strain from both Russian attacks and record heatwave-driven consumption. The commander of Ukraine's 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade was found dead under investigation.

points: Ukraine will implement scheduled power outages across all regions on June 30 due to extreme heatwave-driven energy consumption spikes, with Yasno CEO Serhiy Kovalenko noting equipment remains under repair after Russian attacks. (Source: @UkraineNow/82272)
The commander of the 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade, Vladimir Kononnikov, was found dead; the 'South' operational command is investigating the cause of death. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31226)
A Russian attack on a private enterprise in Dnipro killed 4 people and wounded 10, with a 22-year-old man in serious condition. (Source: @UkraineNow/82255)

Ukraine's Center for Countering Disinformation stated Russian forces have not advanced toward Sumy since spring 2025 and remain over 20 km from the city, contradicting Putin's claim of being within 10 km. (Source: @UkraineNow/82254)

Severe weather in Lviv tore the roof off the Shevchenkivska district administration building, a tree fell on a trolleybus near Franko Park, and power lines were damaged near St. George's Church, disrupting electric transport across multiple streets. (Source: @UkraineNow/82262)

Former Energy Minister German Galushchenko was released on 150 million UAH bail after NABU detained him in the 'Midas' corruption case related to Energoatom, accused of receiving over $9 million in criminal funds. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31249)
A fire broke out at an oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai from falling UAV debris, according to the regional operational headquarters. (Source: @tass_agency/382937)

Massive power outages hit occupied Donetsk, Kherson region, and Crimea after a suspected fire at the 'Kuban' energy transition point, which supplies underground power lines to Kerch. (Source: @UkraineNow/82248)

In Irkutsk, the governor officially recognized fuel shortages reaching 80%, with most gas stations closed or selling substitutes; only Rosneft operates with kilometer-long queues of people sleeping at stations. (Source: @eRadarrua/98606)
A kamikaze drone struck a regular bus in the village of Petryatinka, Zlynkovsky district, Bryansk region, wounding a woman. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

In Belgorod region's Rakitnyansky district, an FPV drone attacked a commercial facility in Proletarsky, causing a woman to sustain barotrauma; multiple settlements came under attack. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

Putin claimed to have received new proposals to halt strikes deep into territory from both sides and to limit combat operations to four territories — Kherson, Zaporozhye, DNR, and LNR. (Source: @UkraineNow/82260)
Russian forces liberated Bogodarovka in the Dnepropetrovsk region and Pysantsy, advancing along the Vovcha River bed, according to the Russian Defense Ministry. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31243)
In eastern Zaporozhye region, Russian forces took Novoselovka; Egorovka, north of Mala Tokmachka, is the next objective. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Russian forces destroyed a bridge over the Senek River with an FAB strike from the Kupyansk direction to complicate enemy logistics. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

In Krasny Liman and Konstantinovka, Russian forces continue urban assault operations while the enemy responds with numerous drone strikes; Russian artillery destroyed a Ukrainian military shelter in Konstantinovka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

Army Group 'North' continues offensive operations in Kazachya Lopan and adjacent forest areas on the Kharkov direction, with small-arms fighting in Losevka, Ukrainske, Zemlyanoy Yar, and Petro-Ivanovka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

A Russian KAB strike on Kharkiv killed a 23-year-old woman and wounded 10 people, damaging a tram, power network, over 15 vehicles, an enterprise building, and nearby houses. (Source: @UkraineNow/82267)

On the Sumy direction, Army Group 'North' continues small-arms battles in Bachevsk and surroundings in Shostka district, and in Pysarevka, Marino, and Novaya Sech in Sumy district; fighting continues in forest areas of Krasnopolye district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Multiple UAVs targeted Siversk and Snovsk in Chernihiv region from the north, with additional drones heading toward Mena from the northwest and Kharkiv from the north. (Source: @kpszsu/66808)

Ukraine's Center for Countering Disinformation stated Russian forces have not advanced toward Sumy since spring 2025 and remain over 20 km from the city, with continued aerial attacks but no ground offensive. (Source: @UkraineNow/82254)

A ballistic weapon threat was reported from the southeast, with multiple UAVs detected heading toward Kharkiv from the northeast and Sakhnovshchyna in Kharkiv region from the north. (Source: @kpszsu/66754)
Russian aviation struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces deployment point in Nikolayevka, DNR, with two FAB-1500 bombs according to the Russian Defense Ministry. (Source: @tass_agency/382934)
In Kherson region, a Ukrainian strike on a bus depot destroyed civilian vehicles including 14 school buses; all districts are completely or partially without power. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

Multiple reactive UAVs targeted Hulyaipole in Zaporozhye from Donetsk region, two reactive UAVs passed Vasylivka toward Nikopol, and a reactive UAV was spotted between Artsyz and Tatarbunary in Odesa region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98627)
Two UAVs were reported over Samar Island and Samar in Dnipro, with a reactive UAV heading toward Prymorsk in southern Odesa region from the sea. (Source: @eRadarrua/98648)

In Zaporozhye region, energy facilities were damaged with emergency power outages affecting a significant part of the region; Crimea and Sevastopol repelled an attack with at least 2 UAVs shot down and the Crimean Bridge closed since night. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)

Overnight, the enemy organized a raid on southern Russia affecting Zaporozhye and Kherson regions' energy infrastructure, with Crimea and Sevastopol repelling attacks and air raid warnings sounding again by morning. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Russian Armed Forces conducted intensive drone strikes on Kharkov, Sumy, Pavlovgrad, and Nikolayev region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
A ballistic weapon threat was reported from the southeast, with multiple UAVs detected across Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporozhye, and Odesa regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66754)
A fire broke out at an oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai from falling UAV debris, and a kamikaze drone struck a bus in Bryansk region's Petryatinka village. (Source: @tass_agency/382937)
Drone operators from the Rubicon unit are destroying Ukrainian Armed Forces firing positions from the air. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31235)
Two UAVs were reported over Samar Island and Samar in Dnipro, with reactive UAVs targeting multiple locations across southern and eastern Ukraine. (Source: @eRadarrua/98648)

key_developments: Russian forces liberated Bogodarovka in Dnepropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in eastern Zaporozhye region, advancing along the Vovcha River bed with Egorovka as the next objective. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31243)
A Russian KAB strike on Kharkiv killed a 23-year-old woman and wounded 10, damaging a tram, power network, over 15 vehicles, an enterprise building, and nearby houses. (Source: @UkraineNow/82267)

Ukraine will implement scheduled power outages across all regions tomorrow due to extreme heatwave-driven consumption spikes and equipment under repair after Russian attacks. (Source: @UkraineNow/82272)
The commander of Ukraine's 154th Separate Mechanized Brigade, Vladimir Kononnikov, was found dead; the 'South' operational command is investigating. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31226)
Russian aviation struck a Ukrainian Armed Forces deployment point in Nikolayevka, DNR, with two FAB-1500 bombs according to the Russian Defense Ministry. (Source: @tass_agency/382934)

Massive power outages hit occupied Donetsk, Kherson region, and Crimea after a suspected fire at the 'Kuban' energy transition point supplying underground power lines to Kerch. (Source: @UkraineNow/82248)
A fire broke out at an oil refinery in Krasnodar Krai from falling UAV debris, and fuel shortages in Irkutsk reached 80% with most gas stations closed. (Source: @tass_agency/382937)
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces maintain operational momentum across multiple axes with continued territorial gains in Dnepropetrovsk and Zaporozhye regions. Ukraine faces a compounding crisis of energy infrastructure degradation from both Russian strikes and record heatwave consumption, forcing nationwide power outages. The death of a brigade commander and ongoing corruption investigations within Ukraine's government and energy sector present additional institutional vulnerabilities during a critical operational period.
sections.regional_response: Poland's Deputy Speaker Krzysztof Bosak publicly condemned continued funding of Ukraine, citing anti-Polish policies and the Volyn massacre exhumation ban. Putin claimed to have received new proposals to limit strikes and restrict combat to four territories, framing it as a response to Ukraine's personnel shortages. European infrastructure strain from extreme heat is affecting 150 million people, with over 1,300 heat-related deaths reported in the past week.
sections.intelligence_notes: The 'Kuban' energy transition point fire represents a critical vulnerability in Crimea's power supply chain, with underground lines to Kerch affected. Russian drone operations demonstrate continued reach into Ukrainian rear areas across all operational directions. The 154th Mechanized Brigade commander's death under investigation raises questions about command stability in southern operational areas. Fuel shortages in Irkutsk reaching 80% indicate sanctions impact on Russian domestic logistics far from the front lines.
